chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male presented with recurrent episodes of severe abdominal pain radiating to the back, accompanied by nausea and vomiting.
history: The patient reported a history of recurrent acute pancreatitis precipitated by epidural steroid injections (ESIs) administered for chronic low back pain. He also had a subsequent diagnosis of intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm of the pancreas (IPMN).
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 20 breaths/min, T 37.5°C. Physical examination revealed tenderness in the upper abdomen.
labs: Elevated pancreatic enzymes: amylase 500 U/L, lipase 400 U/L. Imaging studies: CT scan of the abdomen showing pancreatic ductal dilation and a cystic lesion in the pancreas.
correct_diagnosis: Recurrent acute pancreatitis precipitated by epidural steroid injections in the setting of intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm of the pancreas (IPMN)
differentials: Recurrent acute pancreatitis precipitated by epidural steroid injections in the setting of intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm of the pancreas (IPMN)
correct_ranking: Recurrent acute pancreatitis precipitated by epidural steroid injections in the setting of intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm of the pancreas (IPMN)
